title: Redesign renderAgentCard: 2-line title, merged kind+tab caption, kind-monogram watermark
action: In web/src/views/switcher.ts's renderAgentCard function (currently ~lines 106-121): per D1, change the .agent-path text from row.display to row.title, falling back to row.kind when row.title is empty (mirror the same fallback Snapshot::display_for already applies server-side, just re-applied here against the raw title field). Per D2, remove the separate .agent-kind and .agent-tab spans and replace with one caption span whose text is `${row.kind} · ${row.tab_label}` when row.tab_label is non-empty, or just row.kind when it is empty (same omission rule the current conditional already has, just merged into one element). Per D3, leave the status-badge markup exactly as it is today - do not touch it. Per D4, add a new pure exported function (e.g. kindAccentColor(kind: string): string) that deterministically hashes the kind string to a stable HSL hue (0-359) and returns a CSS color string (e.g. `hsl(${hue}, 45%, 50%)`) - same input always produces same output, no lookup table, works for any kind string including ones never seen before. Render a low-opacity monogram (row.kind's first character, uppercase) as a background decoration inside the card, using kindAccentColor(row.kind) for its color, marked aria-hidden="true" (purely decorative, must never be read by a screen reader or be a tab target). The watermark must sit visually behind the card's text content (title/caption/status badge all stay fully legible on top of it) and must not overflow the card's rounded corners (add overflow:hidden and position:relative to .agent-card in styles.css if not already present). In styles.css, change .agent-path from single-line ellipsis (white-space:nowrap) to a 2-line clamp (display:-webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp:2; -webkit-box-orient:vertical; overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis), remove the now-unused .agent-kind/.agent-tab single-purpose rules or repurpose one of them for the merged caption (avoid leaving dead CSS), and add new rules for the watermark and its low opacity. Add a unit test to web/test/switcher.test.ts (following the existing groupByWorkspace test pattern in the same file) asserting kindAccentColor returns the same value for the same input across multiple calls, and returns a syntactically valid hsl(...) string for at least one never-seen-before kind string (e.g. "gpt5" or "unknown-agent").

truths: The agent card's primary text line shows the raw title (or kind, if title is empty) - never the old kind+title combined display string - and can wrap onto a second line instead of being cut to one line
Every card shows exactly one textual mention of kind (in the merged caption), not two (title no longer prefixes it)
The caption shows "kind · tab" when tab_label is present, or just "kind" (no dangling separator) when tab_label is empty
The status badge is visually and structurally unchanged from before this cell
kindAccentColor(kind) returns the same color for the same kind string on every call, and produces a valid CSS color for any string input, including kinds not seen in this codebase today
The watermark is present in the DOM but marked aria-hidden and does not intercept clicks (the card's existing click-to-select behavior on the button element still works)

artifacts.substantive: renderAgentCard uses row.title with kind-fallback, one merged caption element, a new exported kindAccentColor function, and renders the aria-hidden watermark
.agent-path uses a 2-line clamp instead of single-line ellipsis; watermark styling added; no dead unused selectors left from the old .agent-kind/.agent-tab split
unit tests for kindAccentColor covering determinism and an unseen-kind input, no stub/TODO

key_links: renderAgentCard calls kindAccentColor(row.kind) to color the watermark - not a separate hardcoded color list
prohibitions: Do not change the status-badge markup, classes, or styling
Do not touch groupByWorkspace, renderWorkspaceSection, or any workspace-grouping logic
Do not use a hardcoded per-kind color lookup table - the color must be derived by hashing the kind string
Do not let the watermark intercept pointer events or be reachable by keyboard focus

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: renderAgentCard now shows row.title (kind-fallback, 2-line clamp) and a merged kind/tab caption, with status-badge untouched and a new hashed kindAccentColor watermark; verify green (typecheck + vitest, 20/20).
